note: Big Five personality traits measured via TIPI (Ten-Item Personality Inventory). Direct scoring without T-score conversion - normalized to 0-100 scale.

O.name: Openness
O.description: Imagination, curiosity, preference for novelty and variety. High scorers are creative, open-minded, and intellectually curious.

C.name: Conscientiousness
C.description: Self-discipline, organization, goal-directed persistence. High scorers are reliable, organized, and hardworking.

E.name: Extraversion
E.description: Energy, sociability, assertiveness, tendency to seek stimulation. High scorers are outgoing, energetic, and enjoy social interaction.

A.name: Agreeableness
A.description: Compassion, cooperation, trust, altruism. High scorers are kind, empathetic, and value getting along with others.

N.name: Neuroticism
N.description: Tendency toward emotional instability, anxiety, moodiness. High scorers experience negative emotions more frequently; low scorers are emotionally stable.

scoringFormula.note: For each trait: (1) Average the two items (reverse-scoring where needed), (2) Normalize to 0-100 scale using ((avg - 1) / 6) * 100
